cmd/jobs/billing.purpose: Cobra parent command that aggregates four billing sub-commands (advance, advancecharges, collect, subscriptionsync) under a single 'billing' namespace. Acts as a pure aggregator — contains no business logic itself; all service access flows through internal.App fields wired by app/common.

patterns.name: Aggregator-only parent command
patterns.description: billing.go registers sub-commands in init() and exposes a single Cmd var. No RunE, no flags, no logic.

patterns.name: Sub-commands in separate packages
patterns.description: Each sub-command (advance, advancecharges, collect, subscriptionsync) lives in its own sub-package imported and registered in billing.go's init().

patterns.name: internal.App singleton for service access
patterns.description: All sub-commands access billing services exclusively through internal.App fields (BillingAutoAdvancer, ChargesAutoAdvancer, BillingSubscriptionReconciler) — never construct services locally.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Every RunE uses cmd.Context() — never context.Background() or context.TODO().

patterns.name: Nil guard for optional features
patterns.description: advancecharges guards every execution path against app.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil because charges are optional and wired conditionally.
patterns.example: if app.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil { return errors.New("charges feature disabled") }

patterns.name: PersistentFlags on owning Cmd for shared filters
patterns.description: Shared flags like --namespace belong on the sub-command that owns them, never duplicated across siblings or pushed up to the billing parent Cmd.

patterns.name: Structured input types from domain packages
patterns.description: Sub-commands (collect, subscriptionsync) use typed input structs from domain packages rather than loose primitives to stay compatible with service API evolution.

key_file_guides.role: Pure aggregator — registers the four sub-commands in init() and exposes Cmd for the parent jobs command to mount.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add RunE, flags, or business logic here; this file must remain a zero-logic aggregator.

key_file_guides.role: List, advance-single, and advance-all sub-commands via BillingAutoAdvancer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses nil slice (not empty string) for optional namespace filter — do not default to empty string.

key_file_guides.role: Charge advance sub-commands; nil-guards ChargesAutoAdvancer before every call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Omitting the nil guard causes a panic when the charges feature is disabled (credits.enabled=false).

key_file_guides.role: Invoice collection sub-commands using billingworkercollect structured input types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always supply time.Now() for CollectionAt — passing nil is a known debt in InvoiceCmd, do not replicate it.

key_file_guides.role: Subscription-to-invoice sync reconciliation sub-commands via BillingSubscriptionReconciler.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always apply a lookback filter; omitting it scans all historical subscriptions and is extremely slow.

anti_patterns: Constructing billing or charges service instances locally instead of using internal.App fields
Using context.Background() or context.TODO() instead of cmd.Context() in any RunE
Registering flags on the billing parent Cmd that belong only to a specific sub-command (causes flag pollution)
Hardcoding namespace strings instead of accepting them via a --namespace flag
Omitting the nil guard on app.ChargesAutoAdvancer — panics when the charges feature is disabled

decisions.decision: billing.go is a pure aggregator with no logic
decisions.rationale: Keeps sub-command concerns isolated; billing.go only wires them together so each can evolve independently without touching siblings.

decisions.decision: All service access goes through internal.App
decisions.rationale: Prevents duplicate wiring and ensures the same Wire-provisioned instances (with correct feature flags, including credits.enabled guard) are used in all sub-commands.

decisions.decision: Each sub-command lives in its own package
decisions.rationale: Allows independent flag registration, testability, and import without pulling in unrelated Cobra command state from siblings.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new billing sub-command with optional feature guard

cmd/jobs/ledger.purpose: Cobra parent command grouping ledger administrative sub-commands (currently only backfillaccounts). Owns the ledger namespace in the jobs CLI and coordinates sub-packages that intentionally bypass Wire DI to construct concrete ledger adapters when credits.enabled=false would otherwise produce noop implementations.

patterns.name: Aggregator-only parent command
patterns.description: ledger.go registers sub-commands in init() and exposes Cmd — no RunE, no flags, no logic.

patterns.name: Bypass DI for concrete ledger stack
patterns.description: Sub-commands build concrete ledger adapters directly (not via app/common Wire outputs) because the Wire-provided resolver is a noop when credits.enabled=false.

patterns.name: Flags in init(), logic in RunE
patterns.description: All flag bindings go in init(); RunE contains all execution logic including time flag parsing.

patterns.name: RFC3339 time parsing with UTC normalization
patterns.description: Time-valued flags are parsed from RFC3339 strings inside RunE and explicitly normalized to UTC.
patterns.example: t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, flagVal); t = t.UTC()

patterns.name: Print summary before returning error
patterns.description: Backfill commands print a result summary (processed/failed counts) before returning any error so partial results remain visible to operators.

patterns.name: Interface-driven service in separate service package
patterns.description: Backfill orchestration logic lives in a separate service sub-package with interface-driven dependencies, enabling unit testing without a real database or Cobra context.

patterns.name: Cursor pagination with MAX_SAFE_ITER guard
patterns.description: All paginated loops in the service package use paginationv2.Cursor and enforce the MAX_SAFE_ITER limit to prevent infinite loops on large customer datasets.
patterns.example: for iter := 0; cursor != nil && iter < paginationv2.MAX_SAFE_ITER; iter++ { ... }

key_file_guides.role: Pure aggregator — registers backfillaccounts.Cmd in init() and exposes Cmd for the parent jobs command.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add RunE, flags, or business logic to ledger.go.

key_file_guides.role: Cobra command file: flag binding in init(), concrete adapter construction and RunE execution logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must build lockr.NewLocker — accountservice.New requires a Locker for CreateCustomerAccounts. Do not use the Wire-provided ledger resolver (it is noop when credits.enabled=false).

key_file_guides.role: Pure Go backfill orchestration with no Cobra or Ent imports; interface-driven for unit testability.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use ValidationIssue error codes to detect missing-account condition — not nil checks. DryRun must only increment WouldProvision counters, never write.

key_file_guides.role: Ent-backed implementation of the CustomerLister interface used by the service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must use cursor pagination — not offset — to handle large customer sets safely.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests for the backfill service using mock/fake implementations of CustomerLister and account service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use t.Context() — not context.Background() — so cancellation is tied to the test harness.

anti_patterns: Using the Wire-provided ledger account resolver from app/common — it is a noop when credits.enabled=false
Omitting lockr.NewLocker when constructing the account service — causes a panic at CreateCustomerAccounts
Using offset pagination instead of cursor pagination in large customer loops
Returning an error from RunE before calling printSummary — partial results become invisible to operators
Importing *entdb.Client directly in service.go — breaks unit testability

decisions.decision: Build concrete ledger adapters directly instead of using DI outputs
decisions.rationale: app/common wires ledger services to noops when credits.enabled=false; backfill jobs genuinely need real ledger writes, so they must construct the concrete stack themselves.

decisions.decision: Separate service package from Cobra command
decisions.rationale: Isolates orchestration logic from CLI plumbing, enabling unit tests that do not require a real database or Cobra context.

decisions.decision: Detect missing-account condition via ValidationIssue error codes, not nil checks
decisions.rationale: Ledger adapters return typed ValidationIssue errors for missing accounts; nil-checking Get results is unreliable and breaks idempotency of the backfill.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new ledger sub-command that requires concrete adapter construction
